title: paspalum infirmum
intro: Paspalum infirmum, also known as weak grass or yellow grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Paspalum infirmum include its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as its use as a diuretic and to treat respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be applied topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
